critic_raw:
Issue 1: The baseline performance figures come from the historical ops dashboard anchored to the nightly batch settlement feed. The challenger migration runs on a real-time authorization stream with deduplication. These are different transaction feeds, different populations, and different timing relative to the fraud event.

Issue 2: The 60-day maturation rule applied to the challenger evaluation excludes unsettled disputes. The baseline figures were generated from the full dispute stream where pending and settled disputes both contributed to labels over time. The positive-label rates and population difficulty differ.

Issue 3: The operating threshold was maximized on Q4 data and evaluated on Q4 data. This is circular and inflates precision at the selected volume.

Issue 4: The graph model and the authorization-stream pipeline are deployed together. This bundling prevents attribution of the precision gain to the model alone.
defender_raw:
The authorization stream is the production feed the graph detector will use. Evaluating on the production feed is appropriate. The settlement batch was used historically because the incumbent was designed for it.

The 60-day maturation window is an improvement in label quality. Pending disputes are not confirmed fraud.

The entity resolution audit (500 sampled merges) documented the data quality improvement.

The defender concedes Issues 3 (threshold self-selection) and 4 (bundled model-and-pipeline change). A properly controlled test would fix the feed and label definition for both systems and use predeclared thresholds.
adjudication_raw: The critic raises four issues. The defender concedes Issues 3 and 4 and makes partial rebuttals on Issues 1 and 2 (production realism, label quality). The feed mismatch and label maturation issues are structural — the baseline numbers cannot be directly compared to the challenger numbers regardless of whether each choice was defensible in isolation. Verdict: critique_wins.
all_issues_raised: Issue 1: transaction feed changed from settlement batch to authorization stream
Issue 2: label maturation changed from full dispute stream to 60-day settled-only
Issue 3: Q4 threshold tuned and reported on same Q4 data
Issue 4: model class and pipeline changed simultaneously
